I started when I was 17 lol
oh yeah I forgot ameli doesn't think a funky grid snap is a gimmick
I didnt see the 18 clear, can you go back please
I think sugar would appreciate it
if i explained you wouldnt remember
i may remember my name but never how to do a double diamond
4 double diamond in a row plus a diamond
2.5 screens of needle to be exact
what color would you say those screens would be, seth?
that is what makes the game artsy
olive green, salmon pink, cyan blue
that’s an important one to add too
all of the colors will blend to be a gradient
you ever think about how we call salmon a pinkish orange color because that's what their meat is colored like, even though their skin is silvery or red
it'd be like if we called the color human a deep red for oxidized blood
OH WAIT I’m sorry, replace the color cyan blue with the color “olo”
it’s a new color scientists discovered last year
I’m not making this up btw
you know the eye has 3 color receptors right
you can do it amelia! lock in!
ok the green range overlaps blue and red
well, the only way you can see this color is by getting a laser shot into your eye
so naturally when you see a green signal you also get some red and blue signal
I thought that was just called supersaturated green
but if you get a laser to hit only the green receptor you see green signal without red and blue signal
the only way to see supersaturated green is to stare at blue and red light for so long that it makes your eyes tired and weakens the receptors
wait ameli did you beat floor 17? I didn't see it so I'm not sure
we almost had no blue led, did you know
no you can't imagine it!! that is green with blue and red overlap!!
color stuff like that won't work on a YouTube video
your monitor can only display so many colors
you don't get it pure green stimulates blue and red receptors
you need to fuck up or ignore the red and blues
oh well for that yeah, but supersaturated green =/= olo
it is a brain perception thing
olo is managing to activate just the green receptors without the blue or red ones
unlike red and green, blue light requires a high-energy, large-bandgap material that was incredibly difficult to grow into high-quality crystals
there's a reason stop lights are what they are
Hello i am enjoying a popsicle 😋
I’m just imagining the YouTube olo simulation just being some 90s esque image of some smiling kid sitting down at a computer getting rainbow lasers vigorously shot into their eyes
this onje has no riddle or joke
